titleStmt.title: Letter from Mr Houlihan to the Under Secretary, 10 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Houlihan

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Houlihan to the Under Secretary regarding medical certificates and statements from certain members of staff in connection with the Easter Rising.Sir Robert Chalmers was sent to Ireland as replacement under secretary for Sir Matthew Nathan, who had resigned along with Augustine Birrell, his chief secretary, following the outbreak of the Easter Rising in 1916.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
National Health Insurance Commission, Dublin. 10th May, 1916. Sir, In continuation of their letter of the 6th instant,
I am directed by the National Health Insurance Commission
(Ireland) to supplement the observations contained therein
with the following further particulars regarding certain
members of the staff:â A medical certificate is forwarded, herewith, in the
case of Boy Messenger McGinn; and statements from the folâ
lowing members of the staff are also enclosed, namely: â Mr. John Houlihan, Secretary.
Mr. R. Sheridan, Examiner.
Mr. M. O'Connor, Second Division Clerk.
Mr. J. J. Paisley, Do. Do.
Mr. J. Haughey, Assistant Clerk.
Mr. T. J. Gibbs, Temporary Clerk.
Mr. W. MacKevitt, Do. Do.
Mr. T. Kelly, Do. Do.
Miss M. Kelliher, Woman Clerk.
Miss A. Ryan, Card Teller.
Miss D. Brennan, Typist.
Miss T. O'Loan, Do.
Miss A. McCorry, Do.
Miss G. Smyth, Do.
Miss A. Shott, Do.
Miss G. Sheehan, Temporary Female Clerical Staff.
Miss N, Gannon, Do. Do.
Miss K. Healy. Do. Do. In the case of two assistant Clerks, Mr. F. J. Shouldice
and Mr. T. Cotter, special inquiries have been made, as the
Commission have some reason for thinking that the two
officers mentioned may have had some connection with the
Irish Volunteers. The two special reports on
those officers made by members of the staff who were
specially deputed to investigate personally their case are
attached. The Under Secretary,
DUBLIN CASTLE.
No further information has been obtainable in the
caseof Mr. E. Maguire, Temporary Clerk, Mr. P. Moore, Messenger,
and Mr. T. Croke, Porter, special reports regarding whom
accompanied Mr. Kent's letter of the 6th instant. One of the temporary female staff, Miss M.E. Pender, is
at present resident at 'Kilavalla', Borrisokane, Co. Tipperary,
and has furnished a medical certificate, dated the 3rd instant,
recommending sick leave for 10 days. I am, Sir,
Your most obedient Servant, LM Houlihan
